diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with nasal speech and progressive dysphagia after receiving a therapeutic dose of onabotulinumtoxinA (Botox) for hyperhidrosis. Clinical examination revealed weakness in bulbar and upper limb muscles. Electrodiagnostic findings, including repetitive nerve stimulation (RNS) and needle electromyography (EMG), were consistent with a neuromuscular junction disorder compatible with subacute botulism. The patient's symptoms and diagnostic tests supported the diagnosis of iatrogenic botulism.
